statement.focus_question: Let $p_1<p_2<\cdots$ be a sequence of primes such that $p_{i+1}\equiv 1\pmod{p_i}$. Is it true that\[\lim_k p_k^{1/k}=\infty?
statement.normalized: Let $p_1<p_2<\cdots$ be a sequence of primes such that $p_{i+1}\equiv 1\pmod{p_i}$. Is it true that\[\lim_k p_k^{1/k}=\infty?\]Does there exist such a sequence with\[p_k \leq \exp(k(\log k)^{1+o(1)})?\]
statement.original: Let $p_1<p_2<\cdots$ be a sequence of primes such that $p_{i+1}\equiv 1\pmod{p_i}$. Is it true that\[\lim_k p_k^{1/k}=\infty?\]Does there exist such a sequence with\[p_k \leq \exp(k(\log k)^{1+o(1)})?\]
